name: Gotify Self-Hosted Push Notification Server
what_it_does: Self-hosted push notification server with REST API for sending notifications to Android devices and web clients — runs as a lightweight Go service with SQLite, supports application tokens, and provides a WebSocket stream for real-time message delivery.
use_cases: Sending push notifications from home automation or server monitoring to Android devices
Real-time alerting from CI/CD pipelines without relying on external services
Lightweight notification system for self-hosted infrastructure teams
Agent-to-device notification delivery with complete data sovereignty
Building developer notification workflows from scripts with simple REST API
not_for: iOS push notifications — Gotify Android-only via native app
best_when: A developer needs self-hosted push notifications to Android with full data control, no third-party dependency, and simple REST API integration.
avoid_when: You need iOS support, hosted service, or production-grade consumer push notifications.

auth.notes: Application tokens for message sending. Client tokens for message receiving. Admin token for server management. Tokens scoped to specific applications. No OAuth — simple token-per-application model.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free and open source (MIT license)
pricing.notes: MIT licensed open-source software. No cloud offering. Self-host on any server. Docker image available. Lightweight — runs on Raspberry Pi with minimal resources.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — self-hosted, limited by server resources

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec published. Simple REST API with minimal endpoints. Message sending is a single POST request. WebSocket for real-time message streaming. Error responses are JSON.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All message sends are idempotent by design — each creates a new message record. No state management required.
known_agent_gotchas: Android-only native client — no iOS support without third-party clients
Self-hosted requirement — must run and maintain server infrastructure
Message persistence limited by SQLite database size — configure retention policy
No built-in TLS — requires reverse proxy (nginx/Caddy) for HTTPS

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted means full control over security posture. No native TLS — requires reverse proxy. Application token model is simple but effective. MIT license allows full code audit. No external data sharing.
